chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.565 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.2967/2026 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.2968/2026 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application ------------------------- 24.02.2026 Mr. Muhammad Iqbal Mastoi, advocate a/w applicants ------------------------------------ 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. Applicants/accused Saddam Hussain son of Mazar, Ali Gul son of Mazar, Mazar son of Ali Nawaz and Attaullah son of Mazar Solangi seeks protective bail in FIR No.77/2024, registered at P.S. Tharu Shah, District Naushahro Feroze for offence under sections 302, 324, 506/2, 114, 452, 504, 147, 148, 149, PPC. Learned counsel for applicant submits that applicants are innocent and they have been falsely involved in this case for mala fide intentions and ulterior motives; that they are law abiding citizens and intend to surrender before the concerned Court but they apprehend their arrest at the hands of concerned police in order to humiliate and disgrace, hence they need protection in order to enable them to appear before the concerned Court. Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicant above named in the aforesaid FIR, for a period of five days from today, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand) each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicants are directed to surrender before the concerned Court in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith.

Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicant above named in the aforesaid FIR, for a period of five days from today, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand) each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicants are directed to surrender before the concerned Court in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of five days from today or whenever the applicants surrender before the concerned Court, which one occurs earlier. The applicants are cautioned that this concession of protective bail will not be extended for any reason whatsoever and if they fail to surrender themselves before the relevant Court during validity of this protective bail, their bail bond shall stand forfeited. Instant Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of in the foregoing terms. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
